name: AXS Failed Break Repair v1
edge_mechanism: failed breakdown reclaim that must hold before continuation entry
thesis: AXS 1h is the best structurally different lane for testing failed-break repair, but expressed as a broader reclaim-and-hold structure rather than the already-killed strict failed-breakdown-hold grammar. In a mixed intraday backdrop, downside excursions should fail often enough to produce tradable repairs back into value.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed-break repairs remain too sparse, if repaired moves do not travel enough after costs, or if AXS volatility only adds unusable noise.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below short-term support, quickly reclaims value, proves the reclaim can hold, and then extends modestly as the failed break repairs back toward balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: A choppy intraday regime should create more failed breakdowns than clean downside trends. AXS exaggerates those failures enough to make the mechanism testable if the grammar is broad rather than ceremonial.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is suitable because failed breaks and sharp repairs are visible on a volatile lane without dropping into microstructure noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Failed breaks may bounce weakly but never extend enough after entry, leaving the repair thesis too weak after costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trigger but travel is short, reduce target ambition before making the reclaim condition more selective.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A decent number of repair trades with front-loaded exits carrying most expectancy and little need for long runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 1h plus one cleaner cross-lane show real repair density and enough post-cost travel after reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or repairs that die immediately after re-entry.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakdown, reclaim, hold, and directional handoff are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reclaim logic rather than exact hold choreography

validation_basket.why_included: Best expressive lane for failed-break repair in the active basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Repair trades show enough density and post-cost travel to matter.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS only adds noise or the pattern is still too sparse.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sharp failed breaks, quick reclaim, then a modest repair leg back toward balance.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives on a cleaner major rather than only a volatile lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows the same repair shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The idea only appears on AXS and disappears elsewhere.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner failed-break repairs than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same failed-break repair grammar survives on the slower adjacent timeframe.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves the repair shape without collapsing into no-trade behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The slower lane becomes too sparse.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner repair moves than 1h.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.1 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.1 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
